paragraphs: Researchers have observed a phenomenon known as the Flynn Effect: a consistent increase in Intelligence Quotient (IQ) scores from one generation to the next. This trend, named after intelligence researcher James Flynn, shows that average IQ scores have risen by about three points per decade in many parts of the world.

Various theories have been proposed to explain this trend. One hypothesis is that improvements in nutrition and healthcare have enhanced brain development, leading to higher IQ scores. Another theory suggests that the complexity of modern life, with its increased cognitive demands, has driven this rise. The widespread use of technology and a more stimulating environment could also be contributing factors.

However, the Flynn Effect does not necessarily indicate that people are becoming more intelligent. Some researchers argue that the rise in IQ scores reflects better test-taking skills or familiarity with the types of tasks presented in IQ tests, rather than a genuine increase in cognitive abilities. Recent studies have shown that the Flynn Effect may be slowing down or even reversing in some countries, challenging the notion of a universal and perpetual increase in intelligence.

questions.question: The word "consistent" in the passage is closest in meaning to
questions.special_content: Researchers have observed a phenomenon known as the Flynn Effect: a <mark>consistent</mark> increase in Intelligence Quotient (IQ) scores from one generation to the next. This trend, named after intelligence researcher James Flynn, shows that average IQ scores have risen by about three points per decade in many parts of the world.

questions.question: All of the following are mentioned in the passage as possible contributors to the trend of IQ scores EXCEPT

options.A: better eating habits and medical care
options.C: the cognitive demands of contemporary life

questions.question: According to some researchers, the Flynn Effect may be a sign that

options.A: people are becoming better at taking tests
options.B: IQ tests have become easier over time
options.D: school systems have developed better examinations

questions.question: Why does the author mention that the Flynn Effect may be slowing down or reversing in some countries?

options.A: To suggest that there may not be a global increase in intelligence
options.B: To argue that cognitive abilities are declining around the world
options.C: To show that the Flynn Effect requires further research
options.D: To argue that the challenges of modern life can have negative effects on intelligence

questions.question:
There are four locations [■] in the passage that indicate where the following sentence could be added.

Changes in educational practices or shifts in societal values could account for the downturn.

Where would the sentence best fit? Select a location [■] to add the sentence to the passage.
questions.special_content: [■] However, the Flynn Effect does not necessarily indicate that people are becoming more intelligent. [■] Some researchers argue that the rise in IQ scores reflects better test-taking skills or familiarity with the types of tasks presented in IQ tests, rather than a genuine increase in cognitive abilities. [■] Recent studies have shown that the Flynn Effect may be slowing down or even reversing in some countries, challenging the notion of a universal and perpetual increase in intelligence. [■]
questions.inserted_sentence: Changes in educational practices or shifts in societal values could account for the downturn.

paragraphs: Coral reefs are defined as an underwater ecosystem formed by colonies of tiny marine animals called coral polyps. They are among the most diverse ecosystems on Earth; however, they are facing severe threats from climate change, pollution, and overfishing. These threats have led to widespread coral bleaching, where corals lose their vibrant colors and turn white. Bleaching weakens corals, making them more susceptible to disease and ultimately leading to their death.

Scientists and environmentalists are working on various restoration techniques to combat this decline. These efforts highlight the urgency of finding effective solutions to protect these vital ecosystems. One promising method is coral gardening, where fragments of healthy coral are grown in underwater nurseries and then transplanted onto damaged reefs. This technique has shown significant success in certain areas, helping to rebuild reef structures and promote biodiversity. Another innovative approach involves using electricity to stimulate coral growth. This method, known as Biorock™ technology, creates an electric field that encourages the deposition of minerals found in seawater, accelerating coral growth.

Despite these efforts, challenges remain. Restoration projects are often expensive and labor-intensive, and the long-term success of these techniques is still uncertain. Continuous research and adaptive management strategies are crucial for ensuring the resilience of coral reefs in the face of ongoing environmental changes.

questions.question: What can be inferred about coral bleaching in the first paragraph?

options.B: It is caused by underwater nurseries.
options.C: It is a sign that corals are unhealthy.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a threat to coral reefs in the passage?
Why does the author mention Biorock™ technology in the second paragraph?

options.A: To describe one cause behind coral bleaching
options.B: To introduce an innovative method for coral restoration
options.C: To explain how electricity affects coral health

questions.question: What is one challenge associated with coral reef restoration mentioned in the last paragraph?

options.A: It requires a lot of water.
options.B: It is costly and requires a lot of work.

questions.question: What can be inferred about the long-term success of coral restoration techniques?

options.A: It is guaranteed with current methods.
options.C: It is unaffected by environmental changes.
